---
title: "d-morrison/gha"
subtitle: "Central, reusable GitHub Actions for R-package and Quarto repositories"
---

This site documents [`d-morrison/gha`](https://github.com/d-morrison/gha),
a set of reusable GitHub Actions shared across the
`d-morrison`, `UCD-SERG`, `ucdavis`, `UCLA-PHP`, and `UCD-IDDRC` owners.
It is modeled on
[`r-lib/actions`](https://github.com/r-lib/actions) and
[`easystats/workflows`](https://github.com/easystats/workflows):
a repo calls a shared workflow with a tiny stub instead of carrying its own copy.

The repo is **public** so it can be referenced from repositories across those owners.

## How it works

Each capability ships as two layers:

- A **composite action** (for example `check-bibliography-dois/action.yml`)
  bundles the real steps and any helper script.
  It is referenced as `d-morrison/gha/<name>@v1`.
- A **reusable workflow** (`.github/workflows/<name>.yml`, `on: workflow_call`)
  wraps the composite, declares permissions, and checks out the caller's repo.
  This is what consumer repos target.

A consumer repo adds a small caller stub
(see the [`examples/`](https://github.com/d-morrison/gha/tree/main/examples) directory):

```yaml
name: Check Bibliography DOIs
on:
  push: { branches: [main] }
  pull_request:
  workflow_dispatch:
jobs:
  check:
    uses: d-morrison/gha/.github/workflows/check-bibliography-dois.yml@v1
```

Pin to `@v1`, a moving major tag updated as fixes land.
Do not reference `@main` from consumers.

{{< include _actions-vs-workflows.qmd >}}

## Where to go next

- [Workflows](workflows.qmd) catalogs every reusable workflow and what it does.
- [Permissions](permissions.qmd) lists the `GITHUB_TOKEN` permissions and secrets each one needs.
- [Versioning](versioning.qmd) explains the moving `@v1` tag and how third-party actions are pinned.

This very site is built and shipped by the workflows it documents:
[`quarto-publish`](workflows.qmd#quarto-sites) deploys it to GitHub Pages,
and the [`preview`](workflows.qmd#quarto-sites) family renders a preview for each pull request.
